description_long: This is a short section of a wooden picket fence, featuring five vertical planks of varying heights with pointed tops. The planks are attached to two horizontal rails, one near the top and one near the bottom, providing structural support. The wood is rendered in a medium brown color with visible grain texture, giving it a natural and slightly stylized appearance. The tops of the pickets are cut at angles, creating a jagged, uneven silhouette along the upper edge. There are no visible decorative elements or post caps, and the construction appears simple and functional. This type of fence is commonly used in residential yards, parks, or gardens to demarcate boundaries while maintaining visibility.
description: A stylized wooden picket fence section with five pointed planks of varying heights and two horizontal rails, finished in a medium brown wood texture.
description_view_0: Front view showing five uneven, pointed pickets and two horizontal rails, with the wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the alignment of the planks and rails.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same five pickets and rails, with the wood texture and plank arrangement visible from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the fence and the staggered heights of the pickets.
